title: alnus glutinosa lusus sorbifolia
intro: Alnus glutinosa lusus sorbifolia is a variant of the black alder tree (Alnus glutinosa), a deciduous tree native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Alnus glutinosa lusus sorbifolia include reducing inflammation, soothing irritated tissues, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in the production of essential oils, often in combination with other medicinal plants.
